title: Ezra and the Worried Rainbow Dragon
synopsis: When young Ezra discovers a sad rainbow dragon hiding in his backyard, he learns that even magical creatures need friends. Together, they overcome the dragon's worries about being too different and discover that friendship comes in all colors.

pages.text: Ezra loved playing in his backyard after breakfast. He had curly brown hair and wore his favorite red sneakers everywhere. Today felt special, but he didn't know why yet. As he walked past the big oak tree, he heard a tiny sniffling sound. "Hello?" called Ezra softly. "Is someone there?"

illustration.description: A cheerful boy with warm brown skin and bouncy curly black hair wearing a bright yellow t-shirt and red sneakers stands in a sunny backyard. He's looking curiously toward a large oak tree with textured bark and green leaves. The background shows a cozy house with colorful flower beds and a blue sky with puffy white clouds.
illustration.alt: Ezra, a young boy with curly hair, standing in his backyard looking toward an oak tree

pages.text: Behind the tree sat the most beautiful dragon Ezra had ever seen! She was small, about the size of a puppy, with scales that shimmered like rainbows. But the dragon looked very sad. Big tears rolled down her colorful cheeks. "I'm Zira," she whispered. "I'm scared because I'm different from other dragons. They're all one color, but I'm many colors."

illustration.description: A small dragon with iridescent rainbow scales sits curled up behind the oak tree, looking sad with large tears on her cheeks. Her scales shift through blues, purples, greens, and pinks in the dappled sunlight. Ezra kneels nearby with a concerned, gentle expression. The scene has warm golden light filtering through the tree leaves.
illustration.alt: Zira, a small rainbow-scaled dragon, sitting sadly behind a tree while Ezra approaches with concern

pages.text: Ezra felt sad for his new friend. He sat down next to Zira and gently patted her rainbow scales. "I think your colors are wonderful!" he said with a big smile. "Look around our neighborhood - my friend Maya has beautiful dark skin, and Jin has lovely pale skin. My abuela has silver hair, and baby Kofi has no hair at all! We're all different, and that's what makes us special."

illustration.description: Ezra sits cross-legged next to the rainbow dragon, gently touching her shimmering scales with one hand while pointing toward houses in the distance with the other. In the background, diverse children can be seen playing - a girl with dark skin and braids, an Asian boy, and other children of different ethnicities. The dragon begins to look hopeful, with her scales glowing brighter.
illustration.alt: Ezra comforting the rainbow dragon while gesturing toward diverse children playing in the neighborhood

pages.text: Zira's eyes began to sparkle. "Really? You think being different is okay?" she asked. Ezra nodded enthusiastically. "More than okay - it's amazing! Your rainbow scales are the most beautiful thing I've ever seen. Will you be my friend?" Zira felt so happy that her scales glowed brighter than ever. She spread her tiny wings and gave Ezra the gentlest dragon hug. "Yes! I feel brave now, thanks to you!"

illustration.description: The rainbow dragon Zira is now glowing with happiness, her scales brilliant and shimmering as she spreads her small wings in a joyful pose. Ezra has his arms wrapped around her in a warm hug, both characters beaming with joy. Sparkles of light dance around them, and the background is filled with warm golden sunlight and floating magical sparkles that reflect Zira's happiness.
illustration.alt: Ezra and Zira hugging happily, with the dragon's scales glowing brightly with joy

pages.text: From that day on, Ezra and Zira were the best of friends. They played hide-and-seek among the flowers and shared snacks under the oak tree. Zira learned that her rainbow colors weren't something to hide - they were her special magic! And Ezra learned that the best friendships happen when we help others feel brave and loved, just as they are.

illustration.description: A joyful scene showing Ezra and Zira playing together in the garden. The rainbow dragon flies playfully around Ezra as he laughs and reaches up toward her. Colorful flowers bloom around them, and other diverse neighborhood children can be seen in the background, waving and smiling. The scene is filled with warm sunshine and magical sparkles, creating a perfect happy ending.
illustration.alt: Ezra and Zira playing happily together in a magical garden setting with neighborhood friends nearby

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Zira feel at the beginning of the story?
questions.explanation: Zira was sad and scared because she thought being different made her strange.

questions.question: What made Zira special?
questions.explanation: Zira had beautiful rainbow-colored scales that made her different from other dragons.

questions.question: How did Ezra help Zira feel better?
choices: He told her that being different is wonderful
questions.explanation: Ezra helped Zira by showing her that being different is something special and beautiful.

questions.question: How did Zira feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Zira felt happy and brave because she learned to love her special rainbow colors.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.text: Ezra found a ___1___ dragon behind the tree. The dragon was ___2___ because she was different. Ezra told her that being different is ___3___!

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.text: Zira had beautiful ___1___ scales. At first she felt ___2___, but then she felt ___3___ and brave!
